mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, increasing present equity values and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) during low FEDFUNDS periods, or Rank IC < 0.03 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 8 percent signals excess labor market slack that depresses aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of months when UNRATE exceeds 8.0%, or Rank IC is positive over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous weakness in retail sales and elevated unemployment generate a joint demand drag that exceeds either variable alone, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The bivariate signal produces positive 20-day returns in more than 45% of observations, or the interaction term adds no marginal explanatory power (F-test p > 0.10) in a 3-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: When policy rates are pinned near zero while inflation remains elevated, deeply negative real rates push investors out of cash and bonds into equities as inflation hedges, producing positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
regime_hint: zero lower bound with elevated inflation
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are negative or insignificantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.05) when FEDFUNDS < 0.5% and CPIAUCSL > 220, or Rank IC < 0.02 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below its long-run normalized level indicates contracting manufacturing output and reduced capacity utilization, which compresses corporate profit margins and predicts negative weekly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of weeks when INDPRO is below 100, or the signal Rank IC exceeds 0.00 consistently over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in housing starts and retail sales reflects broad-based demand recovery that single-sector indicators understate, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month through earnings optimism.
failure_condition: The joint HOUST and RSAFS signal fails to outperform the univariate RSAFS signal in directional accuracy over a 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency is below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: Employment growth validated by rising payrolls sustains consumer spending power, so retail sales only predict positive quarterly equity returns when nonfarm payrolls are expanding.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns following high RSAFS readings are not significantly higher when PAYEMS exceeds 130000 versus when it does not (difference-in-means p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels above a critical threshold raise inflation uncertainty and real discount rate expectations, prompting near-term equity de-risking and negative weekly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of weeks when CPIAUCSL exceeds 220, or the Rank IC turns positive over any 2-year rolling window.
